mappings.rationaleAI: The complete biological structure of Homo sapiens organized into organ systems directly corresponding to cardiovascular (A), lymphatic (B), respiratory (C), digestive (D), endocrine (E), reproductive (F), excretory (G), supportive-protective (H), and nervous (J) systems, plus body fluids and tissues (K).
The cardiovascular system comprising heart, blood vessels, and blood (A), with arteries, veins, and capillaries (A.2) and pulmonary and systemic circulation (A.4).
The four-chambered heart with sinoatrial node pacemaker and conduction system (A.1).
Human blood components including plasma (A.3.a), red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets (A.3.a), blood groups (A.3.b), and bleeding and clotting (A.3.c).
The digestive system structure from mouth through large intestine (D.1) and the digestive process including enzymatic breakdown and nutrient absorption (D.2).
The endocrine system including glands and their secretions from thyroid through pancreatic islets (E.1) and endocrine effects upon reproductive processes (E.2).
The central nervous system including brain and spinal cord (J.1), peripheral nervous system including cranial and spinal nerves (J.2), and the autonomic nervous system (J.3).
The brain as the central organ of the nervous system responsible for sensory integration, cognition, and motor coordination (J.1).
Bone composition and properties including cortical and cancellous tissue, osteoblasts, osteoclasts, and mineral homeostasis (H.1).
The muscular system (H.6) with structure and properties of striated skeletal voluntary muscle (H.7.a).
The regulation, control, and dynamics of breathing including chemoreceptor sensitivity and homeostatic regulation (C.3).
The lungs and bronchi as the primary gas exchange organs of the human respiratory system (C.2).
The liver as an associated digestive gland performing bile secretion, detoxification, and metabolic functions (D.1.f).
The integument and derivatives including skin, hair, and nails as part of the supportive-protective system (H.8).
The eye and the process of vision including photoreceptor cells and visual acuity (J.4).
The ear and the process of audition including outer, middle, and inner ear structures and sound transduction (J.5).
Sensory systems including vision (J.4), audition (J.5), and other sensory receptors including cutaneous, kinesthetic, vestibular, gustatory, and olfactory senses (J.6).
Pregnancy involving endocrine control of gestation, parturition, and milk secretion (E.2) and the female reproductive system (F.2).
Female reproductive anatomy including ovaries, fallopian tubes, and uterus (F.2) and female processes under endocrine control including menstrual cycle and menopause (E.2.b).
Male reproductive anatomy including testes and prostate (F.1) and testes as glands affected by endocrine secretions (E.2.a).
Biological sex relevant to the structures and functions of the reproductive system in both males and females (F).
Arthritis as a joint disorder relevant to the structures and functions of the joints (H.3).
Lipids as components of body fluids and tissues including membrane phospholipids and steroid hormones (K).
Homo sapiens anatomy and physiology spanning cardiovascular (A), digestive (D), reproductive (F), and nervous (J) systems.
